goal: 规划 cxs Claude Code adapter 工作流

summary.title: Plan Claude Code adapter workflow
summary.what: Created a durable Codex Session Orchestrator control plane for the next cxs Claude Code adapter phase. The workflow records current source-foundation truth, official Claude CLI/Agent SDK evidence, privacy stop lines, C1-C4 task contracts, C1-C4 starter prompts, deferred E1/R1 gates, verification results, and orchestration noise.
summary.why: The previous multi-source foundation intentionally kept Claude Code reserved and non-public. The next phase needs evidence and design decisions before any parser or adapter implementation, especially because Claude session persistence and transcript shape are privacy-sensitive and current documentation favors official SDK/CLI session surfaces over raw local transcript assumptions.
summary.user_goal: 规划 cxs Claude Code adapter 工作流

decisions.chose: Use docs/workflows/2026-06-05-claude-code-adapter as the canonical control plane.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the Claude adapter phase separate from the completed session-sources workflow while preserving the same orchestrator structure future sessions can read.

decisions.chose: Start with C1 evidence, C2 ingestion-route decision, C3 filtering/linearization policy, and C4 private experimental adapter plan before implementation.
decisions.rationale: Claude support has privacy and schema-stability risks that should be decided before code changes.
rejected: Implement the Claude parser immediately from the handoff
Promote claude-code as a public source in docs or CLI help

decisions.chose: Keep codex as the only public source and keep claude-code reserved/non-public until implementation, tests, docs, and skill behavior are all ready.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the merged multi-source foundation decision and prevents users from relying on unsupported Claude behavior.

decisions.chose: Prefer official SDK/CLI session surfaces for public behavior and treat raw JSONL as private/experimental evidence unless later packets justify otherwise.
decisions.rationale: Current Claude docs expose session/resume/continue behavior and no-session-persistence, while local transcript records can contain private payloads and may drift.

rejected.reason: The handoff explicitly said those worktrees point at old commits and should not be reused for the Claude adapter phase.

rejected.alternative: Fold Claude adapter planning into the completed session-sources control plane
rejected.reason: The previous workflow is complete; a separate control plane avoids stale registry drift and makes the new boundary explicit.

review_notes: Validation: git diff --cached --check passed before commit.
Validation: npm run check passed with 27 test files and 172 tests.
Validation: npm run cxs -- status --source codex --json completed successfully.
Validation: npm run cxs -- status --source claude-code --json returned the expected unsupported_source error.
No runtime, CLI, API, schema, release, global install, push, PR, merge, or production change was made.

architectural_claims: Claude adapter implementation should be preceded by explicit evidence and policy packets.
Claude public behavior should prefer official SDK/CLI session surfaces unless later evidence justifies raw JSONL.
Raw Claude sampling must remain structural-only unless the user explicitly changes the privacy boundary.
security_implications: Adds planning rules that forbid copying Claude transcript text, tool outputs, paths from records, secrets, attachments, snapshots, hook payloads, signatures, thinking, or private payload bodies into docs or handoffs.
migration_notes: No migration was performed. Future implementation must keep claude-code unsupported publicly until accepted.
